industries.name: Retired
industries.note: Largest donor category — retirees contributing individually

industries.name: Securities & Investment
industries.note: Despite populist anti-Wall Street rhetoric, this is Hawley's #3 industry donor
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Hawley brands himself as a populist fighting Wall Street elites while accepting $833K from the securities and investment industry.

industries.name: Real Estate
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Hawley tweets about housing affordability while accepting $738K from the real estate industry that profits from high housing costs.

industries.name: Lawyers/Law Firms
industries.note: Standard donor category for a Yale Law graduate

industries.name: Misc Manufacturing & Distributing
industries.note: Aligns with populist messaging about American manufacturing

industries.name: Leadership PACs
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Hawley positions himself as anti-establishment while accepting $440K from leadership PACs — the establishment's funding mechanism.

industries.name: Health Professionals
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Relevant when Hawley tweets about healthcare costs — his donors include the industries that profit from the current system.

industries.name: Oil & Gas
industries.note: #9 industry donor. Koch-affiliated Americans for Prosperity also spent $4M to elect him in 2018.
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Koch network spent $4M electing Hawley. He consistently votes against climate and environmental measures.

industries.name: Lobbyists
industries.hypocrisyAngle: Hawley talks about draining the swamp while accepting $277K from professional lobbyists.

outsideSpending.hypocrisyAngle: Hawley introduced a bill to overturn Citizens United while being the #1 beneficiary of the exact kind of Super PAC spending Citizens United enabled.
